heading: Define Your MongoDB Search Query
body:
To build your query to return optimal results:

- Select the appropriate aggregation pipeline stage. For example, use
  [$searchMeta](pipe.$searchMeta) if you want to only retrieve metadata. To
  learn more, see [query language](query-syntax-ref).

- Choose an operator that is optimal for retrieving the relevant
  results. For example, consider the [compound](compound-ref) if you want to
  combine multiple clauses such as `must`, `should`, `mustnot` in
  you query. To learn more, see [MongoDB Search operators](operators-ref).

- Apply more processes on your search results by using MongoDB Search
  support for [sorting](sort-ref), [relevance scoring](scoring-ref),
  [faceted navigation](fts-facet-ref), and more.
